input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
       x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       Title="MyMauiApp"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – two sub-tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking for help setting up a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml with a specific navigation structure. This is a code generation/guidance request - I don't need to explore any codebase. Let me create the files they need.

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files showing the structure they described:

1. Flyout with:
   - Dashboard (single page)
   - Products (with two tabs: Active, Archived)
   - Settings page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure breakdown:

**AppShell.xaml** — three `FlyoutItem`s:

| Flyout entry | What it renders |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| **Products** | A `Tab` with two `ShellContent`s (Active / Archived) shown as bottom tabs |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route so you can navigate to it from either product list:

```csharp
// Pass a product ID as a query parameter
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive it with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` on your `ProductDetailsPage` (or `IQueryAttributable`).

**Pages you still need to create:** `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage` — all under the `MyMauiApp.Pages` namespace (matching the `xmlns:pages` declaration).
